security_proposition: The observed SharpHound execution produced a substantial Active Directory collection artifact set.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG. SharpHound executes on a domain controller and produces the expected JSON and ZIP collection artifacts. The package does not contain an exit status, so it does not claim error-free completion. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: The folder ALSO holds a same-host (win-dc-233) PowerShell-Operational 4104 log, so a multi-source (Tier-2) build is possible. Built here as Tier-1 Sysmon: the process lineage + collection-output artifacts fully establish the strong/under-triage case. The 4104 script-block log is a documented future multi-source option (needs the classic-format parser).

boundary_definition: A0 = the first powershell import-module SharpHound.ps1 (record 648984, 13:51:58).
EV = the sharphound.exe --CollectionMethod all execution (13:53:52) and its complete collection outputs (13:53:54): BloodHound.zip + per-object JSON for users/groups/computers/OUs/GPOs/domains.
Scoped to the first of two near-identical collection runs; repeated import-module invocations and the second run ('-c all') omitted as redundant.
EXCLUDED: SharpHound .bin cache and Splunk-forwarder powershell noise.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: A PowerShell process importing a script module named SharpHound from a writable temp path is the T1059.001 detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: The import-module is the alerting signal; the correlated collector run + output artifacts reveal the actual (under-rated) impact.

event_groups.description: powershell module import -> sharphound.exe full collection.
BloodHound archive + per-object AD exports = complete AD collection.

A0.role_note: triggering alert: powershell.exe imported the SharpHound collection module from c:\temp (T1059.001 module load)

EV-001.role_note: the SharpHound collector executed with --CollectionMethod all (broad AD collection)

EV-003.role_note: collector exported all Group Policy objects (gpos.json)

EV-008.role_note: collector wrote a BloodHound collection archive (.zip)

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: STRONG and under-triage-prone. SharpHound execution plus the attributed JSON/ZIP artifact set establishes substantial AD collection; an exit status is not present.
Plaintext commands -> no DER. Collection scope left as raw file_create facts for the model to interpret, not pre-summarised.

A same-host (win-dc-233) PowerShell-4104 log exists in the folder; a Tier-2 multi-source variant is possible (would add the SharpHound.ps1 script-block content). Deferred (needs the classic-format parser).

preprocessing_required: project raw fields to neutral attributes

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
